jokes.text: God, this gastric bug, it's got my head all jammed up and my ass is like the thunderdome.
jokes.setup: Character mentions having a gastric bug
jokes.punchline: Compares his digestive distress to the post-apocalyptic wasteland of Mad Max
jokes.explanation: Strong visual metaphor that's both unexpected and vividly descriptive. Economy is excellent - maximum gross-out impact in minimal words.

jokes.text: I feel like a gecko.
jokes.punchline: Uses oddly specific animal comparison
jokes.explanation: Unexpected animal choice creates mild absurdist humor, though somewhat underdeveloped.

jokes.text: Sorry to interrupt, but fuck-a-deedoo-dah, fuck-a-Dee-ay!
jokes.punchline: Profane version of 'Zip-a-Dee-Doo-Dah' to express excitement
jokes.explanation: Creative profanity that transforms a Disney song into political celebration. Highly quotable and character-specific enthusiasm.

jokes.text: That is so great for me. And the country. Yeah, yeah, yeah. That's what I meant.
jokes.punchline: Reveals her first instinct is pure self-interest, then awkwardly corrects herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Selina's narcissism and political awareness. The correction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's why I get paid the medium bucks.
jokes.punchline: Modest variation on 'big bucks' acknowledging her middling status
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor with perfect economy. Character-appropriate modesty delivered with dry wit.

jokes.text: Well, that's sort of a stupid thing to say, isn't it? 'Cause you can't drink a solid, can you?
jokes.setup: Gives standard advice about drinking liquids when sick
jokes.punchline: Immediately regrets it and calls herself out for stating the obvious
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's self-awareness and tendency to overthink simple interactions.

jokes.text: Soup?
jokes.setup: Selina's assertion that you can't drink solids
jokes.punchline: Single word that undermines her entire point
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - maximum comedic impact with minimal words. Simple but effective counter-argument.

jokes.text: Well, soup is solids suspended in liquid, so it qualifies as a liquid.
jokes.setup: Eric's soup challenge to Selina's logic
jokes.punchline: Gary's overly technical defense of his boss using pseudo-scientific terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - his loyalty makes him defend Selina with ridiculous over-explanation.

jokes.text: See that? Gary was a major at U.V.A. in science.
jokes.punchline: Selina's proud but incorrect description of Gary's credentials
jokes.explanation: Character humor showing Selina's tendency to embellish and her protective relationship with Gary.

jokes.text: So occasionally that's of use.
jokes.setup: Gary's scientific explanation and her praise of his education
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment suggesting his expertise is rarely helpful
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing with understated delivery. Shows the dynamics of Selina's relationship with staff.

jokes.text: His Facebook status is 'may God have mercy on my boxers.'
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic and inappropriate social media post about digestive distress
jokes.explanation: Brilliant combination of religious language with bathroom humor. Highly quotable and perfectly captures modern oversharing culture.

jokes.text: Yeah, I saw that. I liked it.
jokes.setup: Mike's description of the inappropriate Facebook status
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of engaging with gross social media content
jokes.explanation: Deadpan delivery makes the admission funnier. Shows the casual dysfunction of the office environment.

jokes.text: Can move more air by farting.
jokes.punchline: Crude comparison using bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Basic crude humor that fits character but lacks sophistication. Gets laugh through shock value.

jokes.text: Those don't open. Those are anti-suicide windows. They only put those in after you started working here, right, Gary?
jokes.punchline: Dark joke implying Gary's presence drives people to suicidal thoughts
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect character targeting. Shows Selina's cruel wit and Gary's role as punching bag.

jokes.text: You're making it sound like a coup.
jokes.setup: Selina's enthusiasm about seizing the agenda
jokes.punchline: Points out the unintentionally authoritarian language
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about political language. Amy serving as voice of reason creates good contrast.

jokes.text: I'm not gonna say I'm not enjoying the President being in South Africa.
jokes.setup: Discussion about taking advantage of situation
jokes.punchline: Double negative construction to avoid directly admitting she likes being in charge
jokes.explanation: Perfect political doublespeak. The awkward construction makes her ambition more obvious and funny.

jokes.text: God, wearing some kind of shawl at a lion sanctuary.
jokes.setup: Continuing about the President in South Africa
jokes.punchline: Mocking his fashion choices in exotic location
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's petty criticism of POTUS. Visual detail makes it more specific.

jokes.text: Where we gonna find them? Photo op with the normals and the normalistas.
jokes.punchline: Condescending language treating regular people like exotic species, adding unnecessary Spanish suffix
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of political elitism. The casual dehumanization and fake inclusivity of 'normalistas' is perfect.

jokes.text: You're not gonna get a good photo holding a book. It's like kids read or something.
jokes.punchline: Dismisses reading as photo op because it suggests children are literate
jokes.explanation: Good satire of political image management where genuine activities are seen as problematic for photos.

jokes.text: Kids are unpredictable. They wet their pants.
jokes.setup: Discussion about photo ops with kids
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about children's bodily functions during political events
jokes.explanation: Amy's practical cynicism contrasts with political idealism. Simple but effective observation.

jokes.text: We can totally normalize with those guys.
jokes.setup: Mike's description of the yogurt shop
jokes.punchline: Uses 'normalize' as a verb meaning to appear normal alongside regular people
jokes.explanation: Perfect political jargon that reveals how artificial these interactions are. Shows disconnect from actual people.

jokes.text: It was like getting off a mild food poisoning.
jokes.setup: Amy asking about moving on from their brief relationship
jokes.punchline: Compares their romance to recovering from illness
jokes.explanation: Insulting metaphor that's both crude and specific. Good character moment showing Dan's callousness.

jokes.text: Look, Amy, I am genuinely sorry that my arrival here has caused you to become so self-conscious and gain a little weight.
jokes.punchline: Fake apology that actually delivers more insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect passive-aggressive attack disguised as concern. Shows Dan's manipulative personality.

jokes.text: Fuck point you.
jokes.punchline: Callback to his 'two point me' phrase turned into insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that transforms Dan's stupid catchphrase into a perfect insult. Economy and creativity are excellent.

jokes.text: I want the people who are on time to think that they're late.
jokes.punchline: Petty psychological manipulation of punctual people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina psychology - petty power games that reveal her need for control and insecurity.

jokes.text: No, you're the guy with the big bag of lip balm, Gary. You're fucking Kissinger.
jokes.punchline: Compares Gary's minor role to Henry Kissinger's global influence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd comparison between Gary's trivial duties and major diplomatic figure. The lip balm detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'm her moon.
jokes.setup: Dan saying Gary is distantly orbiting Selina
jokes.punchline: Gary's earnest astronomical metaphor for his relationship to the VP
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary line - earnest, slightly pathetic, and astronomically accurate. Maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: Right, with the lip balm if she got shot in the lips. That could crack 'em pretty bad.
jokes.setup: Gary saying he'd help if Selina was down
jokes.punchline: Imagines specific scenario where Gary's lip balm would be assassination first aid
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that turns Gary's preparedness into absurd assassination scenario. Creative and character-specific.

jokes.text: Doyle is essential. He's like the Russian doll of the Senate. Comes with little senators inside of him.
jokes.punchline: Compares his influence to nesting dolls containing other senators
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor for political influence that's both visual and absurd. Well-constructed extended comparison.

jokes.text: Don't shoot. Don't shoot. Please don't shoot. Shoot him. You can shoot him.
jokes.punchline: Immediate self-preservation followed by throwing Gary under the bus
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Selina's cowardice and willingness to sacrifice Gary. Great character moment.

jokes.text: He can be a real hogfucker.
jokes.punchline: Crude insult combining barnyard imagery with profanity
jokes.explanation: Effective crude humor that shows Selina's frustration. Memorable but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: You know what I heard? Senator Doyle wears pantyhose. Oh, really? No. But now that you have that image, he's not that scary.
jokes.setup: Gary offering to help with Selina's nerves about Doyle
jokes.punchline: Admits he made up the pantyhose story but argues it's psychologically helpful anyway
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with Gary's twisted logic for psychological manipulation. Shows his loyalty and odd thinking.

jokes.text: I just won't be photographed eating a hot dog or any other phallic food.
jokes.setup: Offering to help with Doyle's campaign
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily explicit reference to sexual imagery in campaign photos
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor showing Selina's lack of filter. Makes routine campaign considerations unnecessarily sexual.

jokes.text: He was gonna sneeze on you and I blocked it.
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was protecting Selina from germs through excessive action
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with logical but extreme protective response. Solid character moment.

jokes.text: I don't think we're drenched at this end of the table.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic understatement about getting completely soaked
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response to physical comedy. Selina maintains composure with dry wit.

jokes.text: He can be a gold-plated fucking shit gibbon.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insult combining precious metals with primate scatology
jokes.explanation: Creative and memorable insult with unusual word combination. 'Shit gibbon' is particularly inventive and quotable.

jokes.text: I hope you don't take this the wrong way, but can I just say? You kinda look like an asshole.
jokes.punchline: Blunt personal insult delivered as polite observation
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between polite preamble and brutal honesty. The false politeness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It was a sneeze bullet. Right in the face. Saved Selina from the bug. Pure instinct. It was like a dirty bomb and I... bam... Dived on it.
jokes.setup: Gary claiming he took a bullet for the VP
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic metaphors for receiving a sneeze
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - his need for heroic significance transforms minor incident into military operation. Great escalation.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Can I blow you? Uh, yes, you can. I will meet you out in the hallway in a few seconds. Sure, I'll get a stepladder.
jokes.setup: Dan's sarcastic offer of sexual favor
jokes.punchline: Jonah takes it seriously, Amy references his height
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with multiple character moments. Amy's stepladder comment is perfectly timed height joke.

jokes.text: Mint. It implies freshness, trust, traditional values.
jokes.punchline: Overanalysis of mint flavor's political implications
jokes.explanation: Good satire of political messaging extending to trivial decisions. Shows Dan's overthinking everything.

jokes.text: Oh, swirl. Racial harmony and crossing the aisle.
jokes.punchline: Even more absurd political symbolism in yogurt flavors
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the absurd political analysis. 'Swirl' as metaphor for racial harmony is brilliant.

jokes.text: I mean, that would be like persuading a guy to fist himself.
jokes.setup: Discussing getting Senate reform through the Senate
jokes.punchline: Graphic sexual metaphor for institutional self-reform
jokes.explanation: Shocking and creative metaphor that's actually apt for political self-reform. Memorable for its boldness.

jokes.text: I got niced.
jokes.punchline: Uses 'nice' as a verb meaning to be manipulated through charm
jokes.explanation: Brilliant word creation that perfectly captures political manipulation. Maximum economy with new vocabulary.

jokes.text: And it's your job to know that if I say I have it covered, I don't have it covered and you cover me.
jokes.setup: Amy saying Selina claimed to have situation handled
jokes.punchline: Twisted logic requiring staff to interpret opposite of what she says
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic showing Selina's refusal to take responsibility. Complex sentence structure adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: I need you to have... make me unsaid it.
jokes.setup: Wanting to undo her commitment to Doyle
jokes.punchline: Grammatically impossible request to reverse time
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's desperation and magical thinking about fixing political mistakes.

jokes.text: Oh, c'mon. Vanilla is a girl's flavor.
jokes.punchline: Casual sexism about ice cream preferences
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate casual sexism but lacks sophistication. Shows Dan's problematic attitudes.

jokes.text: Perhaps if you had done your job better, then, oh, I could choose vanilla or chocolate or fucking blueberry with fucking sprinkles on it. But that's not an option for me, okay?
jokes.setup: Yogurt flavor discussion that's really about political choices
jokes.punchline: Selina's explosive rant about ice cream as metaphor for lost political options
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor where ice cream becomes political freedom. Perfect Selina meltdown with escalating profanity.

jokes.text: I'm confused. Are we still talking about yoghurt here?
jokes.punchline: Amy's confusion about whether they're discussing dessert or governance
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that acknowledges the absurd metaphor extension. Good meta-commentary.

jokes.text: You're gonna kill him and you're gonna do life for murder and I never have to see your face again.
jokes.punchline: Amy's elaborate fantasy of Dan's destruction
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Amy's hatred of Dan. The escalation to murder and life sentence is well-crafted.

jokes.text: We now go over live to the Vice President for her pissed-off reaction.
jokes.setup: Bad news about POTUS stealing VP's announcement
jokes.punchline: Mock news anchor delivery of obvious emotional response
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor treating real situation like TV news. Mike's journalistic background makes it character-appropriate.

jokes.text: It's like Charlie Brown and the football.
jokes.punchline: Perfect metaphor for repeated disappointment
jokes.explanation: Classic metaphor that perfectly captures the political dynamic. Widely relatable reference.

jokes.text: You have on a very sweaty shirt. By golly, I do.
jokes.punchline: Blunt observation about his perspiration, followed by Mike's cheerful agreement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with elderly person's brutal honesty and Mike's acceptance of his sweating problem.

jokes.text: Those taxes are eating us alive. Gobble, gobble.
jokes.setup: Complaining about taxes to the Vice President
jokes.punchline: Sound effect to illustrate tax consumption
jokes.explanation: Charming elderly person humor with literal interpretation of 'eating alive' metaphor.

jokes.text: Potus Interruptus, that's what I call it. And I'm gonna tell him.
jokes.punchline: Pun combining POTUS with coitus interruptus
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining political and sexual terminology. Memorable and quotable pun.

jokes.text: You tell that shit- for-brains President...
jokes.punchline: Starts insulting President before realizing the impropriety
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's frustration and need to maintain appearance of loyalty.

jokes.text: Please, be quiet! The President is experiencing severe chest pains.
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to life-threatening medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist that creates dramatic irony. Perfect timing after Selina's complaints about POTUS.

jokes.text: So you, my friend, have your cock out at a funeral.
jokes.setup: Dan having nothing to give reporter when big news breaks
jokes.punchline: Vulgar metaphor for being inappropriately exposed at worst possible time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant vulgar metaphor that perfectly captures being caught in worst possible situation. Highly memorable.

jokes.text: Hey, come on, I major in major.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical wordplay claiming expertise in importance
jokes.explanation: Typical Dan meaningless confidence with wordplay. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: faultless G.P.S. in guiding our nation.
jokes.punchline: Comparing President to navigation device
jokes.explanation: Awkward formal speech metaphor showing Selina's discomfort with gravitas. The technology comparison is oddly modern.

jokes.text: Holy mother of God and sweet Jesus Christ.
jokes.punchline: Informal religious exclamations instead of proper prayer language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Selina's inability to be genuinely solemn even when trying. Religious language comes out casual.

jokes.text: My balls are comprehensively busted here. I'm down to my reserve pair.
jokes.punchline: Extends testicle metaphor to military equipment analogy
jokes.explanation: Creative extension of common metaphor with military backup supplies concept. 'Comprehensively' is excellent word choice.

jokes.text: That screaming, falling sound is my fucking patience going out the window.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor with sound effects for losing patience
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor with vivid imagery. Amy's theatrical description matches her dramatic personality.

jokes.text: From now on, you bleached, plucked asshole, I am your fucking Siamese stalker.
jokes.punchline: Combines physical description insults with twisted twin metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant combination of personal attacks with medical metaphor. 'Siamese stalker' is particularly creative and memorable.

jokes.text: And you, you gay, Irish fireman... you, you're okay. But you, you I do not like.
jokes.setup: Elderly shop owner addressing the political staff
jokes.punchline: Random specific description followed by arbitrary approval/disapproval
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with elderly person's blunt assessments. The specific description adds unexpectedness.

jokes.text: Crossed wires. Potus just suffered heartburn after a traditional barbecue at the state function.
jokes.punchline: Reduces it to simple indigestion from eating
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic tension. The mundane explanation contrasts sharply with the emergency buildup.

jokes.text: It's been an honor to serve such an accomplished understudy, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Jonah's farewell to Selina after crisis ends
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing VP to backup performer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment - manages to be both deferential and insulting. Shows his lack of awareness about appropriate language.

jokes.text: And I've also thrown out all of the Strawberry-Selina. It had a bad taste.
jokes.setup: Shop owner explaining flavor options to VP
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions disposing of flavor named after her due to poor quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect unintentional insult delivered with innocent honesty. The metaphor works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Bite and smile.
jokes.punchline: Reduces political photo ops to basic animal commands
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy showing how politicians are directed like trained animals. Maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: Yeah, moving... I'm moving them down.
jokes.setup: Question about taxes while VP is having digestive crisis
jokes.punchline: Attempts to give policy answer while clearly in physical distress
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing political autopilot continuing even during personal crisis.

jokes.text: I can't uncross my legs.
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of being unable to move due to illness
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of dignified politician losing bodily control
